title: -Contract No. 32400104 Amendment: SmartWave for City Facility Managed Wi-Fi

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 32400104 with
Smartwave Technologies LLC, for managed Wi-Fi and related services increasing the
amount by $280,000 for a total not-to-exceed amount of $900,000 from July 25, 2023 to
June 30, 2027.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the additional services in the amount of $280,000 will be available in the
Department of Information Technology’s Cost Allocation Fund (Fund 680), Measure FF
Fund, YAP Parks Tax Fund (Fund 138) and Marina Fund (Fund 608). Planned spending
in the future fiscal years (FY) are subject to Council approval of the proposed city-wide
budget and Annual Appropriation Ordinances.

sections.current_situation:
The City’s workforce continues to shift toward increased mobility and digital collaboration.
Employees now depend on wireless connectivity to access enterprise applications,
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@CityofBerkeley.info Website: http://www.CityofBerkeley.info/Manager

Contract No. 32400104 Amendment: Smartwave for City WiFi CONSENT CALENDAR
December 2, 2025
communicate in real time, and deliver services to the community. As City operations
increasingly utilize cloud-based platforms and mobile devices, reliable and high-
performance Wi-Fi has become essential to maintaining operational efficiency and
consistent service delivery.
To meet these growing demands, the City’s Information Technology Department (IT) has
identified the need to expand Wi-Fi coverage in existing facilities and extend it to new
locations, including the new Fire Department Headquarters, Marina residents, and the
Martin Luther King Youth Center. Additional funding will allow SmartWave Technologies
to deploy new wireless access points (AP’s), enhance performance in high-demand
areas, and complete the initial phases of the Citywide Wi-Fi Project.
SmartWave has demonstrated strong performance and responsiveness, earning IT’s
confidence through consistent delivery, proactive support, and effective management of
over 275 existing APs throughout City facilities. Their continued partnership ensures that
the City can sustain and expand its network infrastructure while maintaining high service
uptime standards.
An enterprise-quality Wi-Fi infrastructure aligns with the City’s Strategic Plan Priority to
provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
In 2023, IT initiated a citywide effort to modernize Wi-Fi infrastructure across all municipal
facilities. As part of this initiative, with Council approval via Resolution No. 70,994-N.S.,
IT entered into a managed services contract with SmartWave Technologies LLC,
leveraging a competitively bid cooperative agreement from the City of Tucson, Arizona.
This partnership has enabled IT to deploy over 275 wireless AP’s across numerous City
facilities, significantly improving network reliability, mobility, and coverage for City staff
and visitors. SmartWave provides turnkey services that include installation, management,
and ongoing maintenance of Wi-Fi networks, which allows IT to ensure high service
availability.
As City operations expand into new facilities, and existing departments adopt more mobile
and cloud-based technologies, there is an ongoing need to expand Wi-Fi coverage to
ensure consistent, secure, and enterprise-grade connectivity across all sites.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Wi-Fi networks provide a more environmentally sustainable alternative to fully wired
Ethernet systems. Deployments require significantly less copper cabling and physical
materials, reducing environmental impact and construction waste. Additionally, as Wi-Fi
enables remote access and digital workflows, it supports operational efficiencies that
reduce travel, energy use, and greenhouse gas emissions.
The flexibility of Wi-Fi also allows for easier office reconfigurations with less material
waste and lower costs over the network’s lifespan. Expanding wireless access across
City facilities will further support community connectivity and digital inclusion goals.
sections.rationale:
Maintaining a citywide Wi-Fi network requires dedicated resources for deployment,
monitoring, and hardware replacement. With more than 275 active access points across
Page 3 of 4
Restricted
Contract No. 32400104 Amendment: Smartwave for City WiFi CONSENT CALENDAR
December 2, 2025
facilities, internal IT staff lack sufficient capacity to provide 24/7 monitoring and rapid
repair response.
Engaging SmartWave Technologies LLC ensures that the City’s Wi-Fi infrastructure
remains reliable, scalable, and cost-effective. Under IT supervision and leadership, the
vendor provides end-to-end management, from installation and maintenance to on-site
support (“truck runs”), ensuring system uptime and minimizing disruptions to City
operations.
The City continues to utilize a competitively bid cooperative contract from the City of
Tucson, AZ (Contract #240021-01, awarded May 10, 2024), which has been adopted by
several other regional jurisdictions, including Oakland, San Jose, Dublin, and Pleasanton.
SmartWave’s proven track record with these municipalities further reinforces the City’s
confidence in their capabilities.
sections.alternatives:
Staff considered completing these installation services internally, but due to the high
volume of existing work, staff do not currently have the capacity or the expertise to
perform such a high volume of Wi-Fi installations and ongoing maintenance at all City
locations.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin C. Fong, Director of Information Technology, (510) 981-6541
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32400104 AMENDMENT: SMARTWAVE FOR
CITY FACILITY MANAGED WI-FI
WHEREAS, the City continues to adopt a more mobile workforce, and the platforms
the City Information Technology Department (IT) provides and supports are
increasingly reliant on wireless technology; and
WHEREAS, via Resolution No. 70,994-N.S., City Council approved the City Manager
to execute a contract with SmartWave Technologies LLC for City facility managed Wi-
Fi; and
WHEREAS, The City of Tucson conducted a competitive Request for Proposal (RFP)
process that solicited bids for a vendor to provide “Region-Wide Point to Point Wireless
System for the Regional Transportation Data Network”; and
WHEREAS, The City of Tucson selected SmartWave Technologies LLC, which was
awarded Contract #240021-01 and accepted on May 10, 2024 with one-year options
to extend on an annual basis; and
WHEREAS, IT has been, and continues to use the competitively bid, cooperative
contract #240021-01 from the City of Tucson, AZ for the same services; and
WHEREAS, to meet increasing demand, IT has identified the need for additional
wireless access points (AP’s) to improve coverage in existing buildings and new sites
due to various city department expansions including the Fire Departments new
Headquarters, Marina and Martin Luther King Youth Center; and
WHEREAS, having an enterprise quality Wi-Fi infrastructure is consistent with the
City’s Strategic Plan Priority to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure,
amenities, and facilities; and
WHEREAS, the projected cost for Fiscal Year 2026 and 2027 is an additional $280,000
in total, which will be available in the IT Cost Allocation Fund (Fund 680), Measure FF
Fund, YAP Parks Tax Fund (Fund 138) and Marina Fund (Fund 608) and planned
spending in future years (FY) are subject to Council approval of the proposed city-wide
budget and Annual Appropriation Ordinances.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Counci
